_meta.purpose: Journal-entry fragments for CaptainsJournal runtime. Consumed by captains_journal.gd. Bias-checked against docs/BIAS_GUARDRAILS.md.

captain_journal: Day 12. The fuel gauge lies. I've started counting by hand.
The crew doesn't talk about the last run. Neither do I.
I found a crack in the port bulkhead. It's been there since launch. We just noticed.
The AI asked me about mercy today. I didn't have an answer.
Three hexes of nothing. Then a signal. Then nothing again.
I'm running out of ways to say 'we're fine.'
The eng tech fixed the recycler with a ration tin and a prayer. It holds.
I dreamed about the genship last night. Woke up and couldn't remember which one.
The Trust invoice arrived. I paid it. What else could I do?
My second asked if I was scared. I said no. I'm a bad liar.
The belt is bigger than the maps say. Or smaller. I can't tell anymore.
We passed a derelict today. The crew wanted to stop. I said no. I don't know if that was right.
The cover test came up clean. I don't know if that's good or bad.
I'm starting to think the AI knows more than it says. I'm right.
Fuel at 40%. Supplies at 60%. Morale: unmeasurable.
The station clerk looked at my registration like it was a death warrant. Maybe it was.
I wrote a letter to my genship. I won't send it. They wouldn't understand.
The crew is bonding. I can see it. I'm on the outside of it.
Another patrol. Another clean sweep. Another day of nothing happening. I'm grateful.
I checked the ledger again. The numbers still don't balance. They never do.
The Trustee's program sent coordinates. I followed them. I always follow them.
My hands shake when I'm alone. They steady when the crew is watching.
The belt doesn't care about my secrets. That's why I'm here.
I met a B1 dispatcher today. She knew my predecessor's name. I didn't.
T5 sent a payroll audit. I passed. I don't know how.
The SAA informant was right about the fuel depot. I owe her.
T4 clinic ship in the area. I'm steering clear. Some debts you don't collect.
The crew found a pre-launch artifact. They wanted to keep it. I said no. Protocol.
I'm tired in a way that sleep doesn't fix.
The hex-map is wrong. I've been correcting it by hand. No one will thank me.
My cover is holding. I think. The AI hasn't said otherwise.
We lost a crewmate yesterday. The ship feels lighter. That's not a metaphor.
I keep thinking about the ones who came before. The ledger knows their names.
The Trust is watching. I can feel it. Or I'm paranoid. Same result.
I made a choice today. I don't know if it was the right one. I never do.
The crew asked about my past. I changed the subject. They let me.
Fuel anxiety is a constant hum. You stop hearing it. Then you can't sleep.
The belt is beautiful when it's not trying to kill you. That's rare.
I found a note from a previous captain. It said: 'Keep going.' I am.
The AI withdrew endorsement today. I don't know why. I keep working.
My genship would be ashamed of what I've done. Or proud. I can't tell the difference anymore.
The station was built by people who never planned to stay. I understand that now.
I'm writing this by hand because the log feels too permanent.
The crew is singing in the cargo bay. I don't know the words. I listen.
Another run. Another ledger entry. Another day of being someone I'm not.
The Trustee's program is working. I think. The dismantling is slow. Everything is slow.
I looked at my reflection today. I barely recognized myself. Good.
The belt took a piece of my ship. I gave it willingly. That's the job.
I'm not the captain they wanted. I'm the captain they got. I'll do.
The last hex before home. I can see the genship lights. I'm not ready.
I kept the crew alive today. That's the only metric that matters.
The AI said: 'You are Captain [N].' I said: 'I know.' I don't.
